[[File:131012 Tokachigawa Onsen Otofuke Hokkaido Japan09bs.jpg|thumb|250px|[[北海道遺産]]のモール温泉]]
'''Tokachigawa Onsen''' (十勝川温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in {{ill|Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja|音曲町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Katō District, Hokkaido|Kato District, Hokkaido]], Japan.
== Spring Quality (Onsen Properties) == The onsen's spring quality includes sodium chloride ([[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]]) and {{ill|bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}, classified as weakly alkaline, hypotonic, and high-temperature.
Millions of years ago, the [[:en:Tokachi Plain|Tokachi Plain]] was a vast inland bay surrounded by wetlands where peat accumulated. This peat transformed into lignite over time, and the onsen water emerges from strata deep underground containing lignite deposits. Tokachigawa Onsen has a unique feature of being a {{ill|Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot spring|ja|モール温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor hot spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モール泉}}, rich in organic matter derived from plants.
== Hot Spring Town == Located along the [[:en:Tokachi River|Tokachi River]] and {{ill|Hokkaido Route 73|ja|北海道道現73号線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Route 73|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hot spring town features several facilities, including {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|ja|道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|1=Roadside station Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30575735}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.jp |title=道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉 |accessdate=2020-10-23}} Other attractions include footbaths and year-round outdoor activities organized by the Tokachi Nature Center.{{Cite web|url=http://www.nature-tokachi.co.jp |title=十勝ネイチャーセンター |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== Events ==
Tokachigawa Swan Festival "Sairinka" (Late January - Late February)
Footpath Walking Event (June)
Flower Landscape "Hanack and Flower Road" (Late June - Late July)
Autumn Festa in Tokachigawa (Early October - Early November)
== Access ==
JR [[:en:Nemuro Main Line|Nemuro Main Line]], Obihiro Station, 20 minutes by car.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.net/access/ |title=アクセス |publisher=Tokachigawa Onsen Tourism Association |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
From Tokachigawa IC (Dōto Expressway), 25 minutes by car.

== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== Events ==
Tokachigawa Swan Festival "Sairinka" (Late January - Late February)
Footpath Walking Event (June)
Flower Landscape "Hanack and Flower Road" (Late June - Late July)
Autumn Festa in Tokachigawa (Early October - Early November)
== Access ==
JR [[:en:Nemuro Main Line|Nemuro Main Line]], Obihiro Station, 20 minutes by car.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.net/access/ |title=アクセス |publisher=Tokachigawa Onsen Tourism Association |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
From Tokachigawa IC (Dōto Expressway), 25 minutes by car.

[[File:131012 Tokachigawa Onsen Otofuke Hokkaido Japan09bs.jpg|thumb|250px|[[北海道遺産]]のモール温泉]]
'''Tokachigawa Onsen''' (十勝川温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in {{ill|Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja|音曲町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Katō District, Hokkaido|Kato District, Hokkaido]], Japan.
== Spring Quality (Onsen Properties) == The onsen's spring quality includes sodium chloride ([[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]]) and {{ill|bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}, classified as weakly alkaline, hypotonic, and high-temperature.
Millions of years ago, the [[:en:Tokachi Plain|Tokachi Plain]] was a vast inland bay surrounded by wetlands where peat accumulated. This peat transformed into lignite over time, and the onsen water emerges from strata deep underground containing lignite deposits. Tokachigawa Onsen has a unique feature of being a {{ill|Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot spring|ja|モール温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor hot spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モール泉}}, rich in organic matter derived from plants.
== Hot Spring Town == Located along the [[:en:Tokachi River|Tokachi River]] and {{ill|Hokkaido Route 73|ja|北海道道現73号線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Route 73|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hot spring town features several facilities, including {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|ja|道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|1=Roadside station Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30575735}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.jp |title=道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉 |accessdate=2020-10-23}} Other attractions include footbaths and year-round outdoor activities organized by the Tokachi Nature Center.{{Cite web|url=http://www.nature-tokachi.co.jp |title=十勝ネイチャーセンター |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== Events ==
Tokachigawa Swan Festival "Sairinka" (Late January - Late February)
Footpath Walking Event (June)
Flower Landscape "Hanack and Flower Road" (Late June - Late July)
Autumn Festa in Tokachigawa (Early October - Early November)
== Access ==
JR [[:en:Nemuro Main Line|Nemuro Main Line]], Obihiro Station, 20 minutes by car.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.net/access/ |title=アクセス |publisher=Tokachigawa Onsen Tourism Association |accessdate=2015-06-15}}

'''Tokachigawa Onsen''' (十勝川温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in {{ill|Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja|音曲町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Katō District, Hokkaido|Kato District, Hokkaido]], Japan.
== Spring Quality (Onsen Properties) == The onsen's spring quality includes sodium chloride ([[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]]) and {{ill|bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}, classified as weakly alkaline, hypotonic, and high-temperature.
Millions of years ago, the [[:en:Tokachi Plain|Tokachi Plain]] was a vast inland bay surrounded by wetlands where peat accumulated. This peat transformed into lignite over time, and the onsen water emerges from strata deep underground containing lignite deposits. Tokachigawa Onsen has a unique feature of being a {{ill|Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot spring|ja|モール温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor hot spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モール泉}}, rich in organic matter derived from plants.
== Hot Spring Town == Located along the [[:en:Tokachi River|Tokachi River]] and {{ill|Hokkaido Route 73|ja|北海道道現73号線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Route 73|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hot spring town features several facilities, including {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|ja|道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|1=Roadside station Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30575735}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.jp |title=道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉 |accessdate=2020-10-23}} Other attractions include footbaths and year-round outdoor activities organized by the Tokachi Nature Center.{{Cite web|url=http://www.nature-tokachi.co.jp |title=十勝ネイチャーセンター |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== Events ==
Tokachigawa Swan Festival "Sairinka" (Late January - Late February)
Footpath Walking Event (June)
Flower Landscape "Hanack and Flower Road" (Late June - Late July)
Autumn Festa in Tokachigawa (Early October - Early November)
== Access ==
JR [[:en:Nemuro Main Line|Nemuro Main Line]], Obihiro Station, 20 minutes by car.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.net/access/ |title=アクセス |publisher=Tokachigawa Onsen Tourism Association |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
From Tokachigawa IC (Dōto Expressway), 25 minutes by car.
== Related Articles ==

This peat transformed into lignite over time, and the onsen water emerges from strata deep underground containing lignite deposits. Tokachigawa Onsen has a unique feature of being a {{ill|Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot spring|ja|モール温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor hot spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モール泉}}, rich in organic matter derived from plants.
== Hot Spring Town == Located along the [[:en:Tokachi River|Tokachi River]] and {{ill|Hokkaido Route 73|ja|北海道道現73号線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Route 73|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hot spring town features several facilities, including {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|ja|道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|1=Roadside station Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30575735}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.jp |title=道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉 |accessdate=2020-10-23}} Other attractions include footbaths and year-round outdoor activities organized by the Tokachi Nature Center.{{Cite web|url=http://www.nature-tokachi.co.jp |title=十勝ネイチャーセンター |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== Events ==
Tokachigawa Swan Festival "Sairinka" (Late January - Late February)
Footpath Walking Event (June)
Flower Landscape "Hanack and Flower Road" (Late June - Late July)
Autumn Festa in Tokachigawa (Early October - Early November)
== Access ==
JR [[:en:Nemuro Main Line|Nemuro Main Line]], Obihiro Station, 20 minutes by car.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.net/access/ |title=アクセス |publisher=Tokachigawa Onsen Tourism Association |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
From Tokachigawa IC (Dōto Expressway), 25 minutes by car.

[[File:131012 Tokachigawa Onsen Otofuke Hokkaido Japan09bs.jpg|thumb|250px|[[北海道遺産]]のモール温泉]]
'''Tokachigawa Onsen''' (十勝川温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in {{ill|Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja|音曲町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Katō District, Hokkaido|Kato District, Hokkaido]], Japan.
== Spring Quality (Onsen Properties) == The onsen's spring quality includes sodium chloride ([[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]]) and {{ill|bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}, classified as weakly alkaline, hypotonic, and high-temperature.
Millions of years ago, the [[:en:Tokachi Plain|Tokachi Plain]] was a vast inland bay surrounded by wetlands where peat accumulated. This peat transformed into lignite over time, and the onsen water emerges from strata deep underground containing lignite deposits. Tokachigawa Onsen has a unique feature of being a {{ill|Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot spring|ja|モール温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor hot spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モール泉}}, rich in organic matter derived from plants.
== Hot Spring Town == Located along the [[:en:Tokachi River|Tokachi River]] and {{ill|Hokkaido Route 73|ja|北海道道現73号線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Route 73|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hot spring town features several facilities, including {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|ja|道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|1=Roadside station Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30575735}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.jp |title=道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉 |accessdate=2020-10-23}} Other attractions include footbaths and year-round outdoor activities organized by the Tokachi Nature Center.{{Cite web|url=http://www.nature-tokachi.co.jp |title=十勝ネイチャーセンター |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}

[[File:131012 Tokachigawa Onsen Otofuke Hokkaido Japan09bs.jpg|thumb|250px|[[北海道遺産]]のモール温泉]]
'''Tokachigawa Onsen''' (十勝川温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in {{ill|Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja|音曲町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Katō District, Hokkaido|Kato District, Hokkaido]], Japan.
== Spring Quality (Onsen Properties) == The onsen's spring quality includes sodium chloride ([[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]]) and {{ill|bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}, classified as weakly alkaline, hypotonic, and high-temperature.
Millions of years ago, the [[:en:Tokachi Plain|Tokachi Plain]] was a vast inland bay surrounded by wetlands where peat accumulated. This peat transformed into lignite over time, and the onsen water emerges from strata deep underground containing lignite deposits. Tokachigawa Onsen has a unique feature of being a {{ill|Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot spring|ja|モール温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor hot spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モール泉}}, rich in organic matter derived from plants.
== Hot Spring Town == Located along the [[:en:Tokachi River|Tokachi River]] and {{ill|Hokkaido Route 73|ja|北海道道現73号線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Route 73|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hot spring town features several facilities, including {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|ja|道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|1=Roadside station Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30575735}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.jp |title=道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉 |accessdate=2020-10-23}} Other attractions include footbaths and year-round outdoor activities organized by the Tokachi Nature Center.{{Cite web|url=http://www.nature-tokachi.co.jp |title=十勝ネイチャーセンター |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== Events ==
Tokachigawa Swan Festival "Sairinka" (Late January - Late February)
Footpath Walking Event (June)
Flower Landscape "Hanack and Flower Road" (Late June - Late July)
Autumn Festa in Tokachigawa (Early October - Early November)
== Access ==
JR [[:en:Nemuro Main Line|Nemuro Main Line]], Obihiro Station, 20 minutes by car.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.net/access/ |title=アクセス |publisher=Tokachigawa Onsen Tourism Association |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
From Tokachigawa IC (Dōto Expressway), 25 minutes by car.

== Hot Spring Town == Located along the [[:en:Tokachi River|Tokachi River]] and {{ill|Hokkaido Route 73|ja|北海道道現73号線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Route 73|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hot spring town features several facilities, including {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|ja|道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|1=Roadside station Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30575735}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.jp |title=道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉 |accessdate=2020-10-23}} Other attractions include footbaths and year-round outdoor activities organized by the Tokachi Nature Center.{{Cite web|url=http://www.nature-tokachi.co.jp |title=十勝ネイチャーセンター |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== Events ==
Tokachigawa Swan Festival "Sairinka" (Late January - Late February)
Footpath Walking Event (June)
Flower Landscape "Hanack and Flower Road" (Late June - Late July)
Autumn Festa in Tokachigawa (Early October - Early November)
== Access ==
JR [[:en:Nemuro Main Line|Nemuro Main Line]], Obihiro Station, 20 minutes by car.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.net/access/ |title=アクセス |publisher=Tokachigawa Onsen Tourism Association |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
From Tokachigawa IC (Dōto Expressway), 25 minutes by car.

[[File:131012 Tokachigawa Onsen Otofuke Hokkaido Japan09bs.jpg|thumb|250px|[[北海道遺産]]のモール温泉]]
'''Tokachigawa Onsen''' (十勝川温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in {{ill|Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja|音曲町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Katō District, Hokkaido|Kato District, Hokkaido]], Japan.
== Spring Quality (Onsen Properties) == The onsen's spring quality includes sodium chloride ([[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]]) and {{ill|bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}, classified as weakly alkaline, hypotonic, and high-temperature.
Millions of years ago, the [[:en:Tokachi Plain|Tokachi Plain]] was a vast inland bay surrounded by wetlands where peat accumulated. This peat transformed into lignite over time, and the onsen water emerges from strata deep underground containing lignite deposits. Tokachigawa Onsen has a unique feature of being a {{ill|Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot spring|ja|モール温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor hot spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モール泉}}, rich in organic matter derived from plants.
== Hot Spring Town == Located along the [[:en:Tokachi River|Tokachi River]] and {{ill|Hokkaido Route 73|ja|北海道道現73号線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Route 73|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hot spring town features several facilities, including {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|ja|道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|1=Roadside station Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30575735}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.jp |title=道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉 |accessdate=2020-10-23}} Other attractions include footbaths and year-round outdoor activities organized by the Tokachi Nature Center.{{Cite web|url=http://www.nature-tokachi.co.jp |title=十勝ネイチャーセンター |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== Events ==
Tokachigawa Swan Festival "Sairinka" (Late January - Late February)
Footpath Walking Event (June)
Flower Landscape "Hanack and Flower Road" (Late June - Late July)
Autumn Festa in Tokachigawa (Early October - Early November)
== Access ==
JR [[:en:Nemuro Main Line|Nemuro Main Line]], Obihiro Station, 20 minutes by car.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.net/access/ |title=アクセス |publisher=Tokachigawa Onsen Tourism Association |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
From Tokachigawa IC (Dōto Expressway), 25 minutes by car.

[[File:131012 Tokachigawa Onsen Otofuke Hokkaido Japan09bs.jpg|thumb|250px|[[北海道遺産]]のモール温泉]]
'''Tokachigawa Onsen''' (十勝川温泉) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in {{ill|Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja|音曲町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Katō District, Hokkaido|Kato District, Hokkaido]], Japan.
== Spring Quality (Onsen Properties) == The onsen's spring quality includes sodium chloride ([[:en:Brine spring|Brine spring]]) and {{ill|bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}, classified as weakly alkaline, hypotonic, and high-temperature.
Millions of years ago, the [[:en:Tokachi Plain|Tokachi Plain]] was a vast inland bay surrounded by wetlands where peat accumulated. This peat transformed into lignite over time, and the onsen water emerges from strata deep underground containing lignite deposits. Tokachigawa Onsen has a unique feature of being a {{ill|Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot spring|ja|モール温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor hot spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モール泉}}, rich in organic matter derived from plants.
== Hot Spring Town == Located along the [[:en:Tokachi River|Tokachi River]] and {{ill|Hokkaido Route 73|ja|北海道道現73号線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Route 73|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hot spring town features several facilities, including {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|ja|道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|1=Roadside station Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30575735}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.jp |title=道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉 |accessdate=2020-10-23}} Other attractions include footbaths and year-round outdoor activities organized by the Tokachi Nature Center.{{Cite web|url=http://www.nature-tokachi.co.jp |title=十勝ネイチャーセンター |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== Events ==
Tokachigawa Swan Festival "Sairinka" (Late January - Late February)
Footpath Walking Event (June)
Flower Landscape "Hanack and Flower Road" (Late June - Late July)
Autumn Festa in Tokachigawa (Early October - Early November)
== Access ==
JR [[:en:Nemuro Main Line|Nemuro Main Line]], Obihiro Station, 20 minutes by car.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.net/access/ |title=アクセス |publisher=Tokachigawa Onsen Tourism Association |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
From Tokachigawa IC (Dōto Expressway), 25 minutes by car.

Millions of years ago, the [[:en:Tokachi Plain|Tokachi Plain]] was a vast inland bay surrounded by wetlands where peat accumulated. This peat transformed into lignite over time, and the onsen water emerges from strata deep underground containing lignite deposits. Tokachigawa Onsen has a unique feature of being a {{ill|Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot spring|ja|モール温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor hot spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モール泉}}, rich in organic matter derived from plants.
== Hot Spring Town == Located along the [[:en:Tokachi River|Tokachi River]] and {{ill|Hokkaido Route 73|ja|北海道道現73号線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Route 73|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hot spring town features several facilities, including {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|ja|道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|1=Roadside station Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30575735}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.jp |title=道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉 |accessdate=2020-10-23}} Other attractions include footbaths and year-round outdoor activities organized by the Tokachi Nature Center.{{Cite web|url=http://www.nature-tokachi.co.jp |title=十勝ネイチャーセンター |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== Events ==
Tokachigawa Swan Festival "Sairinka" (Late January - Late February)
Footpath Walking Event (June)
Flower Landscape "Hanack and Flower Road" (Late June - Late July)
Autumn Festa in Tokachigawa (Early October - Early November)
== Access ==
JR [[:en:Nemuro Main Line|Nemuro Main Line]], Obihiro Station, 20 minutes by car.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.net/access/ |title=アクセス |publisher=Tokachigawa Onsen Tourism Association |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
From Tokachigawa IC (Dōto Expressway), 25 minutes by car.

'''Tokachigawa Onsen''' (十勝川温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja|音曲町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Katō District, Hokkaido|en|Katō District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1134126|lt=Kato District, Hokkaido}}, Japan.
== Spring Quality (Onsen Properties) == The onsen's spring quality includes sodium chloride ({{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring}}) and {{ill|bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}, classified as weakly alkaline, hypotonic, and high-temperature.
Millions of years ago, the {{ill|Tokachi Plain|en|Tokachi Plain|WD=Q11404907|lt=Tokachi Plain}} was a vast inland bay surrounded by wetlands where peat accumulated. This peat transformed into lignite over time, and the onsen water emerges from strata deep underground containing lignite deposits. Tokachigawa Onsen has a unique feature of being a {{ill|Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot spring|ja|モール温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor hot spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モール泉}}, rich in organic matter derived from plants.
== Hot Spring Town == Located along the {{ill|Tokachi River|en|Tokachi River|WD=Q3491135|lt=Tokachi River}} and {{ill|Hokkaido Route 73|ja|北海道道現73号線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Route 73|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hot spring town features several facilities, including {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|ja|道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|1=Roadside station Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30575735}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.jp |title=道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉 |accessdate=2020-10-23}} Other attractions include footbaths and year-round outdoor activities organized by the Tokachi Nature Center.{{Cite web|url=http://www.nature-tokachi.co.jp |title=十勝ネイチャーセンター |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}

{{infobox Onsen | 名称 = 十勝川温泉TOKACHIGAWA MOOR ONSEN | image = 131012 Tokachigawa Onsen Otofuke Hokkaido Japan01s3.jpg | コメント = 温泉街 | 所在地 = [[Hokkaido]][[河東部郡]][[音曲町]] | 緯度度 = 42| 緯度分 = 56| 緯度秒 = 03 | 経度度 = 143| 経度分 = 17| 経度秒 = 46 | 交通 = [[#アクセス]]参照 | 泉質 = ナトリウム塩化物温泉（[[塩化物泉]]）・[[炭酸水素塩泉]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.net/spa/ |title=モール温泉とは |publisher=音曲町十勝川温泉観光協会 |accessdate=2015-06-15}} | 泉温 = | 泉泄量 = | pH = | 液性の分類 = 弱アルカリ性 | 浸透圧の分類 = 低弱性 | 宿泊施設数 = 18 | 総収容人員数 = 4,200{{Cite web|url=http://tokachigawaonsen.jimdo.com |title=十勝川温泉の歴史 |publisher=十勝川温泉旅館協同組合 |accessdate=2015-06-15}} | 年間浴客数 = | 統計年度 = | External links = [http://www.tokachigawa.net/ 音曲町十勝川温泉観光協会] | 特記事項 = }} {{ウィキポータルリンク|温泉}}
[[File:131012 Tokachigawa Onsen Otofuke Hokkaido Japan09bs.jpg|thumb|250px|[[北海道遺産]]のモール温泉]]
'''Tokachigawa Onsen''' (十勝川温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja|音曲町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Katō District, Hokkaido|en|Katō District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1134126|lt=Kato District, Hokkaido}}, Japan.
== Spring Quality (Onsen Properties) == The onsen's spring quality includes sodium chloride ({{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring}}) and {{ill|bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}, classified as weakly alkaline, hypotonic, and high-temperature.
Millions of years ago, the {{ill|Tokachi Plain|en|Tokachi Plain|WD=Q11404907|lt=Tokachi Plain}} was a vast inland bay surrounded by wetlands where peat accumulated. This peat transformed into lignite over time, and the onsen water emerges from strata deep underground containing lignite deposits. Tokachigawa Onsen has a unique feature of being a {{ill|Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot spring|ja|モール温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor hot spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モール泉}}, rich in organic matter derived from plants.
== Hot Spring Town == Located along the {{ill|Tokachi River|en|Tokachi River|WD=Q3491135|lt=Tokachi River}} and {{ill|Hokkaido Route 73|ja|北海道道現73号線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Route 73|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hot spring town features several facilities, including {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|ja|道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|1=Roadside station Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30575735}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.jp |title=道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉 |accessdate=2020-10-23}} Other attractions include footbaths and year-round outdoor activities organized by the Tokachi Nature Center.{{Cite web|url=http://www.nature-tokachi.co.jp |title=十勝ネイチャーセンター |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== Events ==
Tokachigawa Swan Festival "Sairinka" (Late January - Late February)
Footpath Walking Event (June)
Flower Landscape "Hanack and Flower Road" (Late June - Late July)
Autumn Festa in Tokachigawa (Early October - Early November)
== Access ==
JR {{ill|Nemuro Main Line|en|Nemuro Main Line|WD=Q1151791|lt=Nemuro Main Line}}, Obihiro Station, 20 minutes by car.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.net/access/ |title=アクセス |publisher=Tokachigawa Onsen Tourism Association |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
From Tokachigawa IC (Dōto Expressway), 25 minutes by car.
== Related Articles ==

'''Tokachigawa Onsen''' (十勝川温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja|音曲町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otofuke, Hokkaido|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Katō District, Hokkaido|en|Katō District, Hokkaido|WD=Q1134126|lt=Kato District, Hokkaido|1=Katō District, Hokkaido}}, Japan.
== Spring Quality (Onsen Properties) == The onsen's spring quality includes sodium chloride ({{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|WD=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring|1=Brine spring}}) and {{ill|bicarbonate spring|ja|炭酸水素塩泉|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}}, classified as weakly alkaline, hypotonic, and high-temperature.
Millions of years ago, the {{ill|Tokachi Plain|en|Tokachi Plain|WD=Q11404907|lt=Tokachi Plain|1=Tokachi Plain}} was a vast inland bay surrounded by wetlands where peat accumulated. This peat transformed into lignite over time, and the onsen water emerges from strata deep underground containing lignite deposits. Tokachigawa Onsen has a unique feature of being a {{ill|Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot spring|ja|モール温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor hot spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モール泉}}, rich in organic matter derived from plants.
== Hot Spring Town == Located along the {{ill|Tokachi River|en|Tokachi River|WD=Q3491135|lt=Tokachi River|1=Tokachi River}} and {{ill|Hokkaido Route 73|ja|北海道道現73号線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Route 73|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hot spring town features several facilities, including {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|ja|道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|1=Roadside station Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30575735}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.jp |title=道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉 |accessdate=2020-10-23}} Other attractions include footbaths and year-round outdoor activities organized by the Tokachi Nature Center.{{Cite web|url=http://www.nature-tokachi.co.jp |title=十勝ネイチャーセンター |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== Events ==
Tokachigawa Swan Festival "Sairinka" (Late January - Late February)
Footpath Walking Event (June)
Flower Landscape "Hanack and Flower Road" (Late June - Late July)
Autumn Festa in Tokachigawa (Early October - Early November)
== Access ==
JR {{ill|Nemuro Main Line|en|Nemuro Main Line|WD=Q1151791|lt=Nemuro Main Line|1=Nemuro Main Line}}, Obihiro Station, 20 minutes by car.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.net/access/ |title=アクセス |publisher=Tokachigawa Onsen Tourism Association |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
From Tokachigawa IC (Dōto Expressway), 25 minutes by car.

This peat transformed into lignite over time, and the onsen water emerges from strata deep underground containing lignite deposits. Tokachigawa Onsen has a unique feature of being a {{ill|Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot springs|lt=Moor hot spring|ja|モール温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/moor hot spring|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to モール泉}}, rich in organic matter derived from plants.
== Hot Spring Town == Located along the {{ill|Tokachi River|en|Tokachi River|WD=Q3491135|lt=Tokachi River|1=Tokachi River}} and {{ill|Hokkaido Route 73|ja|北海道道現73号線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokkaido Route 73|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hot spring town features several facilities, including {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|ja|道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|1=Roadside station Garden Spa Tokachigawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30575735}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.jp |title=道の駅ガーデンスパ十勝川温泉 |accessdate=2020-10-23}} Other attractions include footbaths and year-round outdoor activities organized by the Tokachi Nature Center.{{Cite web|url=http://www.nature-tokachi.co.jp |title=十勝ネイチャーセンター |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== History == The onsen's historical record dates back to 1874 when it was mentioned in "Hokkaido Chishi Youryo" as "Otogawa Yu." Initially utilized by local Ainu people as "medicine water," the modern history of Tokachigawa Onsen began in 1900 when Kahei Iba redirected lukewarm water into a wooden box for communal use. By 1913, Touzaburo Maeda developed the area, discovering higher-temperature springs through digging efforts.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sasaihotel.com/gensen/ |title=筋温泉物語 |work=Hotel Sasai |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
== Events ==
Tokachigawa Swan Festival "Sairinka" (Late January - Late February)
Footpath Walking Event (June)
Flower Landscape "Hanack and Flower Road" (Late June - Late July)
Autumn Festa in Tokachigawa (Early October - Early November)
== Access ==
JR {{ill|Nemuro Main Line|en|Nemuro Main Line|WD=Q1151791|lt=Nemuro Main Line|1=Nemuro Main Line}}, Obihiro Station, 20 minutes by car.{{Cite web|url=http://www.tokachigawa.net/access/ |title=アクセス |publisher=Tokachigawa Onsen Tourism Association |accessdate=2015-06-15}}
From Tokachigawa IC (Dōto Expressway), 25 minutes by car.
